title: Fix Layout: remove aria-hidden on desktop nav + add aria-labels + add role=menu to dropdown
description:
There are FOUR accessibility issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: Desktop nav has aria-hidden='true' (critical)
The desktop navigation container has `aria-hidden="true"` hardcoded, making ALL navigation links invisible to screen readers on desktop. Find the div with class 'hidden md:flex justify-center mt-4' that has `aria-hidden="true"` and REMOVE the aria-hidden attribute entirely. The JS already manages aria-hidden dynamically for the mobile menu.

### Issue 2: Main <nav> element needs aria-label
The primary `<nav>` element has no aria-label. Add `aria-label="Main navigation"` to the `<nav>` element (the one with class 'border-b border-slate-800 bg-slate-900/50').

### Issue 3: More dropdown needs role='menu'
The More dropdown div (id='more-menu-dropdown') has no `role` attribute. Add `role="menu"` to the dropdown div. Also add `role="menuitem"` to each `<a>` link inside the dropdown.

### Issue 4: Footer needs aria-label
The `<footer>` element has no aria-label. Add `aria-label="Site footer"` to the `<footer>` element.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Fix homepage: search form q parameter not passed through to search page
description:
The homepage has a `<form action='/search' method='get'>` with an input named 'q'. When submitted, it navigates to /search?q=term. But the search page ignores this parameter.

This issue is in the homepage form itself — the form should ensure it works. But the actual fix needs to be in search.astro (which is in the other batch). So for THIS file, verify that the form action and input name are correct. If they are already correct (action='/search', input name='q'), no change is needed for this file.

Actually, skip this — the search page fix is already in the other batch. Instead, fix this: the homepage footer heading hierarchy is broken. The footer uses <h4> elements ('Explore', 'Tools', 'About') but the main content above may end with <h2> or <h3>, skipping heading levels. Change the footer <h4> tags to <h3> for proper heading hierarchy.

Wait — the footer is in Layout.astro which is already being fixed. So instead:

Verify that the `define:vars` on the homepage script tag no longer includes `allPaperIds` (it was removed in a prior round). If it still references allPaperIds anywhere, remove those references.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.
